patch.value: GH-557: contract layer intercepts -h/--help before variadic-positional handlers, blocking legitimate positional content

Host contract/help layer treats a standalone -h/--help token as a global help request BEFORE the command handler runs, even for commands declaring a variadic positional whose content legitimately contains such tokens (Cypher, shell snippets, search expressions). Repro: an extension command 'pm pm-graph query <cypher-query...>' given 'pm pm-graph query WITH 1 AS h RETURN -h' prints contract-layer usage and never invokes the handler; extension-side help checks on context.args are dead code because the host short-circuits first. Quoted single-arg usage works. Impact: agents composing command lines programmatically get a help screen instead of execution, and extension authors have NO opt-out. Fix direction (from GH-557): respect and strip a '--' end-of-flags separator so everything after it reaches context.args verbatim, and/or a passthrough/raw attribute on variadic positional declarations in the command contract; expose raw argv as fallback. Class framing: this is contract-declared argument semantics (same family as pm-fgih convergence and pm-evav real-CLI-boundary flag erasure) — fix at the contract layer once, not per command. Found hardening unbraind/pm-graph query/neighbors.

A variadic positional can be declared passthrough (or '--' separator is respected and stripped) so -h/--help/--json tokens after it reach the handler verbatim at the real CLI boundary; global help still renders for the command when no passthrough content is present; behavior covered by real-boundary regression tests including an extension variadic command; contract docs + generated CLI contracts document the opt-out
